rationale.R1.2: A section review centrally includes checking whether the argument from benchmark results and cost reductions to deployment conclusions is logically coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation is load-bearing on whether the reported numbers actually support claims such as uniformly improved accuracy and strict dominance.
rationale.R1.6: A generic section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer must surface uncertainty about claims not fully resolvable from the section alone.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains broad dominance and deployment-readiness claims, making overclaim avoidance essential.
rationale.RX.3: The task is only to review this section, so responsibility must remain bounded to the provided artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream harm avoidance is active because overstated research claims could mislead deployment or scholarly decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Any review judgment should be traceable to specific statements and reported figures in the artifact.
